blocks.content: After prom the group ended up at Marcus's house. His parents were out of town. The five of them spread across the living room with leftover birthday cake from Marcus's sister's party and a movie nobody was watching.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: No, it's — I just want to say. Tonight was good. All of us, together. This was a good night.
They sat with that for a second. Daisy had her shoes off, legs tucked under her on the end of the couch. Kit was next to her. Their arms were touching.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: August is going to be strange.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I'm just naming it. I think we should name it.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: It is going to be strange.
The movie played. Savannah fell asleep against Marcus. Eli started talking quietly to himself, and then Marcus's eyes closed too, and then there were just two of them awake.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Thank you for tonight. For asking me.
She looked at him in the low light of the TV. He looked back. And then she looked at the movie.
